source.title: Structural Damage - Lifeboat Manual Winch Incident

context.region: Gulf of Mexico - High Island Area Block A-5

actions_taken: USCG transported injured personnel to UTMB Galveston
IP underwent surgery for fractured skull
Contract survival craft specialists scheduled for inspection and training
Development of JSHA for routine tasks including winch operations
Review of operational procedures and checklists
event.summary: On July 11, 2024, at approximately 0730 hours, during a routine life capsule inspection at the High Island A-5 Manta Ray Gathering Facility, a contract pipeline operator from Quality Process Services suffered a serious head injury when struck by a winch assist handle. The Genesis Person in Charge and the injured person lowered the capsule 4 to 6 feet to verify winch operations. After lowering, the PIC engaged the electric winch from the lower section to raise the capsule. The winch assist handle, which should have been removed before operating the electric winch, remained in the socket. As the winch operated, the handle struck the IP in the head, causing him to fall unconscious with head bleeding. A safety device designed to prevent winch operation with the handle in place had been bypassed. The IP was diagnosed with a fractured skull requiring surgery. The incident was attributed to inadequate knowledge of equipment operation and failure to follow safety protocols, with contributing factors including lack of Job Safety Analysis and improper equipment handling.
recommendations: Conduct onsite training for all operators by contract survival craft specialists
Develop JSHA to address safety concerns related to routine tasks including winch operations
Conduct thorough review of operational procedures and checklists to ensure all necessary safety measures are included
key_phrases: winch assist handle remained in place
weekly checklist did not include actual operation of winch
handle should be removed and stored when not in use
IP positioned on upper section where winch located which is unnecessary

hazards.name: Rotating mechanical equipment with stored energy

threats.name: Winch operated with manual assist handle still inserted
Safety interlock bypassed allowing winch operation with handle in place
Personnel positioned in strike zone during winch operation

consequences.name: Loss of consciousness and significant blood loss

controls.name: Winch assist handle removal procedure
controls.lod_basis: Operator action to remove handle before winch operation

supporting_text: The winch assist features a handle designed to be removed and stored when not in use, and it should be removed before operating the electric winch
The winch assist handle was not removed prior to engaging the powered winch in bypass, leading to the incident
Failure to Follow Safety Protocols: The failure to remove the winch assist handle before engaging the powered winch directly led to the incident

controls.name: Safety interlock to prevent winch operation with handle in place
controls.barrier_role: Physically prevent winch from operating when handle inserted
controls.lod_basis: Automatic safety system independent of operator action

supporting_text: A safety to override the handle in place and power the winch was later found to be bypassed
It was learned after the initial investigation that a safety device was bypassed allowing winch to operate
engagement of the powered winch while the winch assist handle was still inserted and in bypass to allow winch to operate

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) for winch operations
controls.barrier_role: Identify hazards and establish safe work procedures

supporting_text: The IP and the PIC opted to use a weekly inspection checklist for this task instead of preparing a Job Safety Analysis (JSA) together with the checklist
Lack of Job Safety Analysis (JSA): The task was considered routine, and no JSA or Job Safety and Health Analysis (JSHA) was conducted
The weekly checklist did not include the actual operation of the winch

controls.name: Weekly capsule inspection checklist
controls.barrier_role: Guide inspection activities and verify equipment condition

supporting_text: The IP and the PIC opted to use a weekly inspection checklist for this task instead of preparing a Job Safety Analysis (JSA)
The weekly checklist did not include the actual operation of the winch
Review of Procedures: A thorough review of operational procedures and checklists should be conducted to ensure that all necessary safety measures are included

controls.name: Personnel positioning during winch operation
controls.barrier_role: Keep personnel clear of strike zone during equipment operation
controls.lod_basis: Operator awareness and safe work positioning

supporting_text: The IP was positioned on the upper section of the davit where the winch is located, which is unnecessary during winch operation
The IP used the winch assist to lower the capsule from the upper deck near the winch
Human Performance Error- Not aware of Hazards

controls.name: Equipment operation training
controls.barrier_role: Ensure personnel competent in safe equipment operation

supporting_text: Human Performance Error- Inadequate knowledge of equipment operation
Training and Awareness: Genesis has scheduled a contract survival craft specialists to inspect the area and conduct onsite training for all operators

controls.name: First aid and emergency response
controls.barrier_role: Minimize injury severity through immediate medical intervention

supporting_text: The PIC called for assistance via handheld radio and began administering first aid to the IP
At 0756 hours, Genesis Health, Safety, Security, and Environment (HSSE) contacted a medivac service company
The USCG arrived at the facility at 0957 hours

controls.name: Medical evacuation capability
controls.barrier_role: Transport injured personnel to advanced medical care
controls.lod_basis: Emergency response capability to access definitive care

supporting_text: At 0756 hours, Genesis Health, Safety, Security, and Environment (HSSE) contacted a medivac service company out of Lake Charles for a medivac
The USCG arrived at the facility at 0957 hours
USCG departed at 1033 hours, transporting both the injured IP and the PIC to University of Texas Medical Branch (UTMB) in Galveston

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
